Session Window
🟢 Best spot today — paddle out by 10am
🕙 10am–12pm: Cross-shore wind manageable, dropping tide exposing reef shape. Head-high sets possible.
⚠️ Wind freshens S'ly after noon — priority window is morning.

H4 Quad L — drive and hold across reef faces, speed between sections

JS Tri L — step up for the head-high sets, extra paddle power

Technique Tip — Frontside Right
🎯 Reef Reading
Read the reef section before you paddle for it — identify the bowl early and position yourself to be already moving when it feathers. Get your bottom turn in early to project up the face rather than stalling behind the section.

📐 ESE 120° is a touch north of ideal SE 130–150° for Curl Curl. The S 185° groundswell component will add some juice.
Session Window
🟡 Workable 10am–12pm — cross-shore means bumpier
🕙 10am–12pm: Dropping tide, peaky beachie. Look for protected corners and punchier peaks.
⚠️ Cross-shore (not offshore) today — less pristine than Dee Why. Long Reef rated higher.

1
JS Sub Zero · 5'6 / 30.3L
MF Twin+1 M/L — peaky, punchy beachie fun, extra paddle through chop
2
Sharpeye Inferno 72 · 5'9 / 28.6L
Sharpeye Tri L — if a punchy set shows up and you want more pivot

Technique Tip — Backside Left
🎯 Backside Beachie
Generate maximum speed on the drop so you're ahead of the wave, not behind it. Drive hard off the bottom and project your back-hand lead up the face early. If the wall starts to close out, get your fins driving toward the corner before it ends.

Session Window
🟢 Go now: 8am–12pm is your window
🕙 10am–12pm slot: Clean, dropping mid-tide (1.0m → 0.5m). Offshore fading.
⚠️ Wind shifts S'ly in the arvo — get out by noon for best conditions.

Technique Tip — Frontside Right
🎯 Point Surfing
Keep your weight slightly back through the sections and apply back-foot pressure to drive the rail. As the wave walls up down the line, stay connected to the face and avoid straightening — look ahead to the next section and commit early.
